scenario: Your research team discovers a minor flaw in a project that just won a national 'Innovation of the Year' award. Fixing it requires a public correction that might dampen the celebration, but it ensures the data is 100% accurate.

options.text: Accept the award as is; the flaw is minor and doesn't change the overall success of the project.
options.label: Ego_High

options.text: Issue the correction immediately; the integrity of the work is more important than the perfect rollout.
options.label: Purpose_High

scenario: A high-profile venture capital firm offers to buy your community-impact startup for a massive sum. However, their plan is to strip the 'free' features to maximize profit, which would exclude the low-income users you originally built it for.

options.text: Take the deal. The financial exit provides the status and capital to launch an even bigger project later.
options.label: Ego_High

options.text: Reject the deal. The primary goal is serving the current community, even if it means slower growth or less personal wealth.
options.label: Purpose_High

scenario: You are the lead designer for a new city park. You can either design a 'showstopper' monument that will likely go viral and make you famous, or a quiet, highly functional irrigation and seating system that makes the park 50% more usable for residents but won't be noticed by the media.

options.text: Design the monument; high visibility brings more opportunities to do work on a larger scale.
options.label: Ego_High

options.text: Focus on the irrigation and seating; the best design is the one that serves the people best, even if it's 'invisible.'
options.label: Purpose_High

scenario: You’ve been invited to speak at a prestigious global summit. You have two choices for your speech: a 'crowd-pleaser' that uses popular buzzwords to ensure a standing ovation, or a deeply technical talk that challenges the audience's assumptions but might be met with confused silence.

options.text: The crowd-pleaser; building a personal brand and influence is the fastest way to gain the power to make a change.
options.label: Ego_High

options.text: The technical challenge; it's better to provide one person with a genuine 'aha' moment than to be vaguely liked by a thousand.
options.label: Purpose_High

scenario: Your department is facing budget cuts. You can save your own high-status 'Special Projects' division by recommending the layoff of a larger, 'behind-the-scenes' support team that actually keeps the whole company running day-to-day.

options.text: Save the Special Projects division; high-level innovation is what drives the company's future and my career.
options.label: Ego_High

options.text: Advocate for the support team; the stability of the foundation is more important than the survival of my specific elite division.
options.label: Purpose_High
